Title: Merbok murder case: Post-mortems completed, identification pending
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Post-mortem examinations have been completed on a married couple found dead in Kampung Belau, Merbok, but their bodies have yet to be released as formal identification is still ongoing.
Kuala Muda district police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said the bodies remain at the Forensic Unit of Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital.
"Although the post-mortems were completed yesterday, the bodies have not been handed over as DNA and fingerprint identification processes are still ongoing," he said.
He added that the body of the suspect — identified as the son of the female victim — had been claimed by his family and was buried yesterday at a cemetery near Masjid Al Atiq in Merbok.
The bodies of three family members were discovered at a house in Kampung Belau earlier this week.
They were identified as Zikri Abdul Rahman, 65, and Suraya Man, 53, as well as Suraya's son, who is believed to be the suspect.
Police said the couple are believed to have died more than 48 hours before they were found, while the suspect is believed to have died more recently.
Investigations are ongoing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 200-litre subsidised RON95 cap: Rakyat should not suffer from govt's shortcomings, says PN
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Perikatan Nasional (PN) has questioned the government over its planned cap on RON95 petrol subsidies, saying it is only a temporary fix that restricts consumption.
Instead, the coalition has proposed a series of measures to cushion rising costs for the public.
PN information chief Tan Sri Annuar Musa said the move to limit RON95 purchases to 200 litres per month from April 1 would burden small traders and the self-employed, who are already facing rising costs due to the global oil crisis.
He added that the government's claim that nearly
90 per cent of Malaysians
would not be affected does not reflect the reality on the ground, particularly for the self-employed, small traders, small-scale logistics operators, and families.
"Perikatan Nasional also questions why the government chose an approach that restricts rakyat's consumption.
"The rakyat should not have to suffer because of the government's administrative weaknesses," Annuar said in a statement today.
Among the measures proposed to ease the impact on the public are gradual price adjustments to avoid sudden shocks, expanding targeted subsidies to cover micro, small and medium enterprises, cooperatives, and sectors such as transport, logistics, and food supply, as well as protecting middle-income groups from being left out.
PN also called for strategic subsidies for essential goods transportation to contain price increases and urged the government to use additional revenue from crude oil exports to temporarily support subsidies.
The coalition highlighted the need for greater transparency in the automatic pricing mechanism (APM) to help the public better understand fuel pricing, alongside stricter enforcement against profiteering.
For the long term, PN proposed building at least a year's oil reserves, diversifying import sources, optimising refining capacity at RAPID Pengerang, and introducing a clear subsidy rationalisation plan based on economic indicators such as inflation and household income.
They also called for broader energy reforms, including reducing reliance on imported refined petroleum and exercising caution over any export ban to ensure economic stability.
The coalition also noted that diesel prices had risen to RM5.52 per litre, placing further strain on transport and supply chains involving micro, small, and medium enterprises.
"Perikatan Nasional emphasises that the people need a comprehensive solution, not just temporary measures that raise concerns, and urges the government to provide assurances that any interim steps will not become permanent policy.
"In this challenging situation, the government must come forward with policies that are more transparent and prioritise the welfare of the people.
"PN will continue to play a constructive role in ensuring that the interests of the people remain a top priority," the statement read.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang weighs WFH to ease impact of rising petrol and diesel prices
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The Penang government is weighing the possibility of adopting a work-from-home (WFH) directive for civil servants following rising fuel costs linked to global conflict.
Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the state would first study the federal government's directive before deciding how best to implement it locally.
"Although this is not new since the Covid-19 pandemic, we will wait for the official directive and consider how to implement it effectively," he told newsmen here today.
He said allowing some civil servants to work from home could help reduce physical commuting and ease the burden of higher transportation costs, particularly in light of escalating petrol and diesel prices driven by ongoing geopolitical tensions.
When asked about the impact of fuel price increases on Penang's industries, Chow acknowledged that both public and private sectors were already feeling the strain.
He cited waste management operations as a clear example, noting that garbage collection contractors rely heavily on diesel-powered trucks.
"Some contractors are allowed to purchase subsidised diesel, while others must use industrial diesel. With the recent price surge, their operating costs have increased significantly," he added.
Chow pointed out that unlike other sectors, waste collection services could not reduce frequency to cut costs.
"Rubbish must be collected daily according to schedule. This means the additional costs will likely have to be borne by local authorities, as contractors are entitled to claim price increases under existing contract clauses," he said.
He also referenced reports that ferry operators in Langkawi had reduced trips due to rising diesel prices, underscoring how fuel costs are forcing operational adjustments in other industries.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had reportedly encouraged the
private sector
to adopt flexible work arrangements, following the government's move to expand such practices within the civil service.
The initiative comes as the government looks to cushion the impact of rising costs linked to global supply disruptions, particularly in fuel and essential goods, following escalating tensions in the Middle East.
Anwar said the initiative, including work-from-home arrangements, would be implemented in stages for civil servants and could help Malaysians better navigate the economic pressures arising from global developments.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran engaging "intimate friends", including Malaysia, to ensure safe Hormuz passage
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Iran said it is actively engaging with its "intimate friends", including Malaysia, to ensure the safe passage of vessels through the Strait of Hormuz.
The Iranian Embassy in Malaysia, in a statement, said this is being done to address the broader economic implications of tensions in the Middle East, following what is described as "military aggression" by the United States and Israel.
It said countries, particularly in Southeast Asia, are already feeling the effects of increased oil prices and disruptions in supply chains arising from perceived threats to the Strait.
The potential for long-term instability, it added, raises concerns about economic security and energy reliability in the regions.
"In light of these developments, Iran is actively engaging with its intimate friends to address the situation and ensure the safe passage of vessels.
"Notably, the Prime Minister of Malaysia, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, recently held a phone conversation with the Islamic Republic of Iran's President Dr Masoud Pezeshkian, to discuss collaborative efforts aimed at cushioning the energy crisis exacerbated by the ongoing conflict.
"Such diplomatic dialogues underscore Iran's commitment to maintaining peace and stability in maritime trade," read the statement.
The Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most important shipping channels, is a vital maritime chokepoint, through which approximately one-fifth of the world's oil and a significant share of liquefied natural gas pass.
The embassy also touched on reports that the US was considering plans to occupy or blockade Kharg Island, home to a major oil terminal in Iran, in a bid to pressure Teheran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
The embassy said that while military control of local facilities like Kharg Island is important, it does not mean control over the entire Strait of Hormuz.
"Iran's coastline, which is approximately 1,500 miles long, allows us to exert influence without direct ownership, so any US ground invasion of Iranian islands would create regional security uncertainty that would disrupt global energy markets," it said.
It said recent assessments also indicate a shift in US objectives, now focusing on gaining access to the Strait of Hormuz. Iran, it noted, is dedicated to the principles of the UN Charter and recognises the importance of maritime security.
"We affirm that non-hostile vessels, particularly those from nations that do not support military aggressions against us, can navigate these waters safely, contingent on compliance with Iranian regulations and coordination with our authorities."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Water levels drop across key Perak dams as dry spell intensifies
Article:
IPOH: Water levels at three major dams in Perak have declined as the state continues to experience prolonged hot and dry conditions linked to the El Niño phenomenon.
Data from the National Water Services Commission (Span) war room portal and the Kerian Department of Irrigation and Drainage (DID) show falling levels at Air Kuning Dam in Taiping, Sultan Azlan Shah Dam in Kinta, and Bukit Merah Dam in Kerian.
Bukit Merah Dam, which is under the supervision of the Perak DID, recorded a water level of 6.98m, or 34.77 per cent capacity, today— down from 7.01m (37.74 per cent) a day earlier.
The level places the dam under the irrigation supply standard operating procedure at Critical Level 1, which is set at 7.01m.
Meanwhile, Sultan Azlan Shah Dam recorded 243.80m, or 95.79 per cent capacity, while Air Kuning Dam stood at 42.20m, or 90 per cent. Both dams, managed by the Perak Water Board, also showed a decline from full capacity recorded the previous day.
The drop in water levels comes as several areas in the state, including Sauk in Kuala Kangsar, have reported supply disruptions in recent days.
In a Facebook post, Perak Menteri Besar's special officer for Chenderoh, Datuk Mohd Ariff Abdul Majid, said the issue in Sauk was linked to falling river levels affecting operations at the Sauk Water Treatment Plant.
He added that concerns had also been raised over unauthorised water abstraction activities upstream, which may be contributing to the problem.
"Feedback from affected communities will be conveyed to Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad, and we will continue to coordinate with relevant agencies to ensure prompt action," he said.
Earlier, the Perak Water Resources Management Board (LPSA) confirmed it had conducted a site visit to the Sauk plant and surrounding upstream areas following complaints from the Perak Water Board.
The inspection found evidence of unauthorised water abstraction, including the installation of intake points to divert water, which could affect river levels and disrupt raw water supply for local residents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 5E Resources set for IPO launch and ACE Market listing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: 5E Resources Holdings Bhd is expected to launch its prospectus and open its initial public offering (IPO) for application on March 30, 2026, with a targeted listing on the ACE Market on April 15, 2026.
The IPO involves the issuance of 304.5 million new ordinary shares. This includes 77 million shares for the Malaysian public, 35 million shares for eligible directors, employees and contributors to the group, and 192.5 million shares via private placement to Bumiputera investors approved by the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry.
In addition, 154 million existing shares will be offered for sale through private placement to selected investors.
Executive director and chief executive officer of 5E Resources, Lim Te Hua, said the registration of the prospectus marks another important milestone as it progresses towards its ACE Market listing.
"We are proud of the progress we have achieved over the years, having built a resilient and compliant business with a strong financial track record. The scheduled waste management industry is underpinned by regulatory requirements, where proper handling and treatment of scheduled waste is mandated by law, providing a stable and recurring demand environment for our services.
"As we move closer to our prospectus launch, we are confident in our growth prospects and excited about the opportunities ahead as we execute our expansion plans to deliver stronger performance in the years ahead," he said.
The IPO proceeds will be utilised primarily for the construction of the company's new scheduled waste management facility in Perak and the acquisition of plant and equipment for the said facility.
The company expects to complete the construction works on the Perak facility in the second half of 2029, with operations targeted to commence in the first half of 2030.
The balance of the proceeds will support the company's operational funding requirements as it continues to scale its business.
TA Securities Holdings Bhd is the principal adviser, sponsor, underwriter and placement agent for the proposed IPO.
Head of Corporate Finance of TA Securities, Ku Mun Fong, said the company has established a solid operating foundation, underpinned by its regulatory compliance, technical capabilities and strong financial performance.
"Operating in a regulatory-driven industry with stable demand fundamentals, and supported by its clear expansion plans, we believe 5E Resources is well positioned for its next phase of growth," Ku said.
5E Resource, via its subsidiaries, is principally engaged in the provision of scheduled waste management services, comprising testing, collection, recovery and recycling of scheduled wastes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysian rights activist banned from entering Singapore
Article:
SINGAPORE: A Malaysian activist who studied in Singapore has been denied entry after allegedly engaging in political activism there.
In response to queries, the (Singapore) Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) said on March 27 that Fadiah Nadwa Fikri had urged some youths to "adopt her brand of radical advocacy."
The ministry added that she encouraged them to go beyond protests, mobilising students and communities in Singapore, and to undertake disruptive or violent actions to support specific causes.
Fadiah has long been active in civil society and campaigning for Palestinian freedom.
She is a member of the Malaysian legal rights advocacy group Lawyers for Liberty and the Centre to Combat Corruption and Cronyism.
Fadiah, who completed a PhD with the National University of Singapore in 2025, had posted on social media platform X on March 22 and 23 that she had been notified that she was banned from entering the country after trying to do so on March 22.
MHA said: "We will not tolerate foreigners getting involved in our domestic politics, nor the promotion of unlawful, violent and disruptive methods of civil protest.
"Fadiah is an undesirable visitor, and we have thus denied her entry into our country," The Straits Times reported.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Education Ministry upholds King's decree on student safety amid heatwave
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Education Ministry upholds the decree of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, to safeguard the safety and well-being of students, teachers and the school community, amid the current hot weather.
In a statement today, the ministry said the King's decree served as a reminder for all educational institutions under its purview to remain vigilant and strictly adhere to established heatwave guidelines.
"The ministry has issued instructions to all institutions under its administration. This includes monitoring outdoor activities and granting special permission for students, teachers, and support staff to wear appropriate sports attire during hot and dry conditions," it said.
The announcement comes as schools prepare to reopen next Monday (March 30) following the first term break.
Under current guidelines, schools are advised to postpone outdoor activities such as cross-country runs, camping, and drills if daily maximum temperatures exceed 35 °C for three consecutive days.
The ministry also empowered school administrators to temporarily close schools if temperatures exceed 37 °C for three consecutive days, following a Level 2 heatwave warning.
Yesterday, Sultan Ibrahim, in a post on his official Facebook page, emphasised the importance of reducing outdoor exposure and ensuring adequate water intake.
His Majesty also urged the ministry to take the matter seriously to ensure that the health of students is not adversely affected by the phenomenon, which is expected to persist until June.
The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has recently issued heatwave alerts for several areas, particularly in the northern states of the Peninsula, as the country enters the final phase of the Northeast Monsoon.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bad season for padi farmers as rice fields fail to yield
Article:
KANGAR: A 14.4ha or 50 relongs of padi field in Sungai Batu Pahat here could not be harvested after facing a lack of water supply when the dry season began at the end of last year.
The situation left padi farmers empty-handed and suffering heavy losses, as yields dropped drastically while they also grappled with rising operating costs.
Padi farmer Masri Abu Hassan, 43, said this season's rice crop was the worst he had ever experienced, as prolonged hot weather meant water could not be channelled into the padi fields.
"Previously, the proceeds from padi sales for each relong (0.29 hectares) of field could reach RM2,600 to RM3,000, but now it is very difficult to even earn RM1,000.
"Once the costs of land rental, harvester wages, seed prices, and tractor and lorry hire are deducted, the farmers' income is not enough to cover their capital.
"Furthermore, the rise in diesel prices has forced wages to be increased to cover the cost of operating the machinery," he said when met today.
For padi farmer Azhari Abu Bakar@Chik, 60, more than half of the fields he cultivated were damaged and could not be harvested this season.
"I cultivated 10 relongs (2.88ha) of padi, but only four relongs could be harvested.
"Even worse, my child's seven-relongs (2ha) field could not be harvested at all because the seeds were sown too late last November.
"I should have earned around RM20,000 this season, but this time I only managed about RM3,000.
"That amount isn't even enough to pay the land rent, let alone cover the cost of seeds, drone fertiliser spreading, and pesticide application. This season has truly been terrible," he said.
Meanwhile, padi farmer Che Razali Abdullah, 58, said that rice-growing areas in the village were severely affected because their irrigation relies entirely on rainfall.
"If the rain does not continue, we simply cannot sow the seeds.
"According to the schedule, we should have started sowing by May, but judging by the current weather, it seems we cannot begin yet.
"If the weather had been favourable earlier, farmers would typically have earned around RM1,500 for each relong (0.29ha) over a six-month period," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 25 years of absolute power: Evolution of Aston Martin Vanquish
Article:
This month (March) marks a quarter-century since Aston Martin unleashed the V12 Vanquish, a nameplate that redefined the modern Super GT.
From its 2001 debut to the current 835PS titan, the Vanquish has remained the high-water mark for British engineering and brute elegance.
The Pioneer: V12 Vanquish (2001-2007)
When it broke cover at the 2001 Geneva Motor Show, the original Vanquish was a technological moonshot.
It was the first Aston to feature a carbon fibre and bonded aluminium chassis - tech developed in collaboration with Silicon Valley and the University of Nottingham.
Under the hood sat a 6.0-litre V12 churning out 460bhp, mated to an F1-inspired paddle-shift gearbox.
As the final model built at the historic Newport Pagnell facility, it bridged the gap between old-school craftsmanship and the digital age, immortalised by its "drive-by-wire" throttle and aggressive silhouette.
The Carbon King: Second Generation (2012-2018)
The second-generation Vanquish arrived in 2012, drawing heavy stylistic cues from the One-77 hypercar.
This iteration was a study in lightweight materials; every exterior panel was crafted from aerospace-grade carbon fibre, making the body 25 per cent lighter than the DBS it replaced.
The 6.0-litre V12 evolved to produce 565bhp, later reaching 600bhp in the "S" variants.
With a 0-100km/h sprint of 4.1 seconds and the introduction of Carbon Ceramic Matrix brakes, it solidified the Vanquish as a world-class performer that did not sacrifice grand touring comfort.
The Modern Masterpiece: Third Generation (2024-Present)
Today's Vanquish is the most potent flagship in the marque's 113-year history.
Launched in 2024, it moved to a 5.2-litre twin-turbo V12 producing a staggering 835PS and 1000Nm of torque.
With a top speed of 344km/h (214mph), it is the fastest series-production Aston Martin ever made.
Beyond the numbers, the current car features a wheelbase extended by 80mm for a more dramatic profile and a bespoke interior that sets new luxury standards.
Limited to fewer than 1,000 units annually, it remains an ultra-exclusive icon.
A Legacy Of Conquest
"Since its arrival 25 years ago, the Vanquish nameplate has been synonymous with something special; something ambitious, different and daring," says Aston Martin chief executive officer Adrian Hallmark.
As the name suggests - to "conquer" or "overwhelm" - the Vanquish has spent 25 years doing exactly that to its rivals.
Whether it's the raw mechanical soul of the 2001 original or the twin-turbocharged ferocity of the 2026 model, the Vanquish remains the definitive symbol of what a front-engined British supercar should be.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Store worker charged with trafficking over 3kg of meth in Tawau
Article:
TAWAU: A 42-year-old store worker has been charged in the Magistrate's Court here with trafficking more than 3kg of drugs earlier this month.
Hadriansyah Sabran acknowledged the charge when it was read before Magistrate Don Stiwin Malanjum, but no plea was recorded as the case falls under the High Court's jurisdiction.
He is accused of trafficking 3,071.04g of methamphetamine at a vehicle storage facility in Lorong Yong Luk, Jalan Sin Onn, Tawau, at about 7.30pm on March 13.
The charge was framed under Section 39B(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, which carries the death penalty or life imprisonment, along with not fewer than 12 strokes of the cane if the death penalty is not imposed.
The prosecution was led by Jeffrey Kadisun, while the accused was unrepresented.
Bail was denied, and the court fixed April 27 for mention and submission of the chemical report.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bukit Kwong dam at alert level amid dry spell
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Bukit Kwong dam remains at alert level, with its storage capacity at 51.7 per cent as of March 25, amid the ongoing dry spell affecting parts of Kelantan.
State Department of Irrigation and Drainage (DID) director Dr Siti Fairus Zakaria said the dam's water level remains within a controlled range but is under close monitoring due to a gradual decline.
"The current level at the dam is still at the alert stage, and we are monitoring the situation closely as the dry conditions persist.
"The percentage was recorded from January to March 25," she said.
According to the department's operational chart, the dam's normal water level is set at 16.76 metres above mean sea level (MSL), while drought alert, warning and danger thresholds range between 15.7m and 14.0m MSL for the January to June period.
Siti Fairus said the Bukit Kwong dam was designed to serve multiple purposes, including flood mitigation, irrigation and agricultural supply, with water levels fluctuating depending on rainfall and inflow.
She added that the dam has a catchment area of 11sqkm, fed by the Tandak Liang River, which flows into the Golok River, the main river in the Golok basin.
"At its normal pool level of RL16.76m, the dam has a storage capacity of about 14.3 million cubic metres, with an active storage of approximately 10.49 million cubic metres," she said.
The Bukit Kwong dam, located in Rantau Panjang, is among the main dams in Kelantan that serve multiple purposes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nik Nazmi, Chegubard in talks to settle flood project defamation suit
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad and activist Badrul Hisham Shaharin are in negotiations to settle a defamation suit over statements linked to the river basin transformation flood mitigation projects.
Nik Nazmi's lawyer, Yiow Ern Syenn, informed High Court judicial commissioner Gan Techiong that on Feb 25, they had sent a letter to Badrul Hisham outlining several settlement proposals.
During the proceedings, Yiow also spoke on behalf of Badrul Hisham's lawyer, Razlan Hadri Zulkifli.
"We received a letter today from the defendant (Badrul Hisham) stating that he had agreed to most of the terms, except for monetary compensation.
"The defendant is requesting more time to negotiate the financial aspect," she said.
Despite the ongoing discussions, Gan stressed that the trial scheduled for May 4 will proceed if no settlement is reached.
"If you settle, well and good. If you fail to settle, the trial must proceed, and witness statements must be filed on or before April 20.
"This case commenced in January 2024, and the court will not allow it to be adjourned.
"If you achieve settlement, write to the court, and I will set an immediate date to record the consent order," he said.
Yiow confirmed that the defendant had been informed that the matter would proceed to trial if an amicable settlement was not achieved.
Nik Nazmi, who is the Setiawangsa member of parliament, is suing Badrul Hisham for alleging that the projects, costing up to RM16.6 billion, were tainted by corruption.
Badrul Hisham claimed that millions in kickbacks were involved in securing the flood mitigation projects, leading to a significant increase in costs.
Nik Nazmi dismissed these allegations, stating that the projects had not ballooned to RM16.6 billion.
Badrul Hisham further alleged that a company registered as a cosmetics firm doubled up as a flood mitigation consultant, siphoning millions from the project intended to address nationwide flooding.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court to hear update on bid to quash Zara inquest on May 25
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The Sessions Court has set May 25 for an update on a judicial review seeking to quash the inquest into the death of Zara Qairina Mahathir.
Judge Amir Shah Amir Hassan set the date during today's Zoom mention for the committal proceedings against former Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Mohd
Shafie Apdal
.
Shafie is alleged to have made comments about the inquest that may amount to contempt of court.
During the previous hearing, on Feb 11, the court stayed the committal proceedings and set today's date for parties to provide an update on the status of the judicial review via Zoom.
Deputy public prosecutor (DPP) Christopher Enteri Mawan, when contacted, said that they had informed the court that the judicial review was still ongoing and had not yet been concluded.
"The court then asked parties to update it on the status of the judicial review in the High Court at the next mention on May 25, via Zoom.
The parties will also discuss the constitutional issues related to an ordinance and the impact on federal and state law," he said.
On Oct 23, last year, Amir granted the Attorney-General leave to commence
contempt proceedings
against Shafie and activist Che Muna Din, also known as Mona Din.
On Nov 21, 2025, leave for judicial review to challenge the ongoing inquest into the death of Zara, claiming that the proceedings were not conducted in accordance with Sabah's inquest laws, was filed to the High Court.
The application, which was filed by Pertubuhan Kebajikan Ekonomi Semangat Bersatu Malaysia (Pembela), named the Attorney-General and the Coroner's Sessions Court as the first and second respondents respectively.
The Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC) had
objected
to the application.
Zara, a Form One student, was found unconscious at her hostel on July 16 last year and was pronounced dead the following day at Queen Elizabeth Hospital.
The inquest into her death began on Sept 3, 2025, and is ongoing.
Christopher, along with DPPs Mohamad Mustaffa P Kunyalam and Nadia Mohd Izhar, represents the A-GC.
Lawyers Jakariya Ab Jalil, Chen Ket Chuin, and Batholomew Jingulam representing Shafie.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] MACC urges formal reports over unverified claims of payments to politician
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has urged anyone with credible information on allegations involving payments to a politician to come forward and lodge an official report.
The graft busters said it has not received any formal complaint related to the claims, which are currently circulating in the media and remain unverified.
"This is to allow investigations to be carried out comprehensively, transparently and based on facts.
"The MACC remains committed to examining all information received, and any action will only be taken based on solid evidence, in accordance with the law," it said.
The MACC was responding to allegations of a RM9.5 million payment said to have been made by businessman Victor Chin Boon Long to a group described as a "corporate mafia".
Chin yesterday said the payment made to the
so-called "corporate mafia" group
was not a bribe, but a "service fee" paid under pressure to stop ongoing harassment.
He said the payment was not made to settle any investigation, close any case or secure any favour from enforcement agencies.
Instead, it was a "service fee" extracted under threat by the syndicate.
Chin also said the group had pressured him to surrender his shares for free and, to force compliance, had targeted those around him by freezing more than 500 bank accounts belonging to individuals and companies believed to be linked to him.
He welcomed the establishment of a Royal Commission of Inquiry or any fair and independent investigation into the matter, but urged that it be conducted thoroughly and impartially, covering all parties involved and not limited to him alone.
He had been at the centre of a growing controversy involving allegations of a so-called "corporate mafia" and an ongoing police investigation.
Chin, who is linked to business interests associated with NexG Bhd — the sole supplier of Malaysia's passport booklets and identity cards — is being investigated under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act (AMLA) over alleged links to the group. He has denied any involvement.
He claimed he and his associates were instead subjected to sustained pressure by the group, alleging it had attempted to gain control of NexG Bhd through intimidation, including threats and repeated demands for the "delivery of shares".
He alleged that instructions related to these demands were conveyed through an intermediary identified as "Mr R", whom he claimed was a member of parliament linked to PKR and who had requested RM10 million in October last year in exchange for resolving the matter.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fuel subsidy cap: Motorists back 200-litre limit as 'fair and manageable'
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Most motorists support the government's move to lower the monthly fuel subsidy limit to 200 litres, saying it is fair and unlikely to affect their daily expenses. Many see it as an opportunity to manage fuel use more responsibly.
The adjustment, announced by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, will reduce BUDI95 subsidy eligibility from 300 litres per month to 200 litres starting 1 April 2026.
A survey by the New Straits Times found many motorists consume below the new threshold and expect little to no impact on their monthly expenses.
Retired supply chain manager Magesan, 59, said the new limit would not affect him as his monthly fuel usage is below 150 litres, but welcomed the policy as a push for more conscious fuel use.
"I don't expect my fuel costs to increase. But if my usage goes above 200 litres, I will cut down on travel and rely more on public transport," he said.
Similarly, teacher Elis, 55, said the policy encourages motorists to plan their movements more carefully.
"We will plan our journeys more carefully and avoid unnecessary travel," she said, adding that she is prepared to adjust her routine, including reducing trips, to stay within the limit.
"If my fuel cost increases, I will accept it. This is our part in supporting the government," she said.
Meanwhile, doctor Naily Athirah, 36, who drives a Myvi, said the policy would not affect her as she does not exceed the limit.
[[nid:1404718]]
"I never reach 200 litres in a month. For users like me, it's fine," she said, noting that heavier users may feel the impact.
She described the adjustment as reasonable, as subsidies are still provided up to a certain level.
"If you use more, you pay more. That's fair," she said.
Technician Razman, 50, said he trusts the government's decision.
"What the prime minister is doing is the best for the country," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heavy congestion reported across key expressways
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic volume on major expressways nationwide has surged as of midday today, with significant congestion and several bottlenecks reported in the northern, southern and Klang Valley sectors.
According to the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) traffic management centre, as of 1.09pm, traffic is moving slowly along several stretches of the North-South Expressway (NSE) E1 in the north.
Congestion has been detected from Permatang Pauh to Perai and Seberang Jaya towards Juru Auto City.
Heading further south, traffic is building up from Changkat Jering towards Kuala Kangsar and at the approach to the Meru Tunnel.
In the southern sector (E2), northbound traffic is experiencing heavy volume from Skudai to Kulai, while a crawl is reported from the Sungai Besi Toll Plaza towards the city centre.
At the Johor Causeway, southbound traffic remains heavy from the Sultan Iskandar Building (CIQ) towards Woodlands.
The heavy volume has been further complicated by vehicle breakdowns.
A lorry is currently stalled at Km240.3 (northbound) between Kuala Kangsar and Changkat Jering, with the left lane obstructed.
Earlier, a trailer breakdown at Km29.0 (northbound) from Kulai to Sedenak was cleared at 12.50pm, though traffic remains sluggish as the high volume of vehicles struggles to disperse.
Similarly, a breakdown at Km262.7 near the Menora Tunnel was resolved at 12.16pm, with traffic returning to a steady but heavy flow.
The rush is also being felt on urban expressways and routes heading to the east coast.
On the Kuala Lumpur-Karak Highway (E8), westbound traffic is slow from Lentang to Bukit Tinggi.
Within the Klang Valley, the Damansara-Puchong Expressway (LDP) is seeing high-volume congestion in areas such as Puchong Utama to Puchong Intan, the PJS Toll Plaza to the Cable Bridge, and Taman Megah towards Damansara Utama.
On the ELITE Highway (E6), motorists are advised to expect delays heading north from USJ to Seafield.
LLM reminded motorists to stay focused, maintain a safe following distance, and ensure they give clear signals before changing lanes.
The end of the school holiday season is the primary driver behind today's massive exodus.
The current traffic surge aligns with earlier projections by Plus Malaysia Bhd, which estimated that over two million vehicles would traverse its network this Friday alone.
This spike represents a significant increase compared with normal working days, as families make their final journeys home or head out for the final holiday weekend, leading to sustained high traffic volume throughout the day.
The public can obtain real-time traffic updates via the LLM hotline at 1-800-88-7752, the TUJU Super App, or by following @llminfotrafik on X. Live CCTV feeds are also accessible at www.llm.gov.my
.

© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hamzah calls for emergency Dewan Rakyat sitting over Middle East conflict impact
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The opposition has called for an emergency Dewan Rakyat sitting to discuss the domestic impact of the escalating conflict involving Israel, the United States, and Iran.
Opposition leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin made the request in a letter dated March 26 to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, which was also copied to the Dewan Rakyat Speaker.
Hamzah said the conflict had caused a sharp spike in global crude oil prices, placing immense pressure on international energy markets and the national economy.
"Opposition members of parliament have received various questions and reactions from the public following the prime minister's announcement yesterday.
"These matters must be discussed constructively and transparently, taking into account the interests of the people and the country's overall economic standing," he said in a statement today.
"On behalf of the opposition bloc, I have sent an official letter to the prime minister seeking consideration to convene a special sitting under Standing Order 11(3) of the Dewan Rakyat as soon as possible," he added.
Hamzah said the session would allow for an open and comprehensive debate to build public confidence in the country's preparedness for current economic challenges, particularly regarding food security.
Under Standing Order 11(3), the prime minister may require the speaker to call a meeting of the house on the grounds of public interest.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: E-hailing group urges review of Budi95 fuel quota, claims 800 litres insufficient
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: An e-hailing group has urged the government to review the Budi95 subsidy mechanism, claiming many drivers are forced to pay market prices for fuel after exhausting their quotas mid-month.
Sahabat E-hailing Malaysia (SEM) said the current 800-litre monthly limit was insufficient for high-mileage drivers, some of whom consume up to 40 litres of petrol a day.
"Even before the Middle East conflict, e-hailing drivers were facing a dire financial situation as fares were inadequate to cover operating costs. Now, some are forced to pay for unsubsidised RON95 starting from the middle of the month after hitting their limit," SEM said in a statement today.
SEM also criticised the different eligibility criteria and quotas set for e-hailing drivers, diesel-based vehicles, and airport taxis.
"The requirement for e-hailing drivers to meet a minimum travel distance is a biased policy that primarily benefits service provider companies," it said.
The group highlighted that many drivers risked losing their additional quotas because the eligibility criteria failed to account for unforeseen circumstances such as accidents or vehicle breakdowns.
"Using the previous month's travel record as a prerequisite is unfair and punishes those who lose access to their vehicles due to accidents or lengthy insurance claims.
"By April, more drivers are expected to lose their additional quotas because they could not meet trip requirements during the Ramadan and festive period," it added.
Under the current tiered system, e-hailing drivers must clock over 5,000km a month to qualify for the maximum 800-litre quota. Those covering between 2,000km and 5,000km receive 600 litres, while those under 2,000km are entitled only to the base quota.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar to convene MBs, CMs over economic situation
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The federal government will convene a meeting with all state leaders next week to address the current global economic pressures stemming from ongoing tensions in the Middle East.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the move was part of measures to mitigate the global impact of the crisis, adding that he also plans to call leaders from all political parties.
He said the meeting would be briefed by the National Economic Action Council, the National Security Council and relevant agencies to provide a clear picture of the current situation and the steps required moving forward.
"Next week, Insya-Allah, I will call all menteri besar, premier and chief ministers, regardless of whether they are from the government or the opposition.
"I also plan to call leaders of all parties to discuss and hear briefings from the National Security Council and the National Economic Action Council on the measures we must take together to address the situation," he told reporters after attending Friday prayers at Masjid Tengku Ampuan Jemaah.
Responding to Opposition Leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin's call for a special Dewan Rakyat sitting to discuss the implications of the Middle East conflict, Anwar said the matter would first be deliberated with state leaders and party heads to formulate a comprehensive plan.
https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/03/1400553/hamzah-tells-govt-table-plan-tackle-rising-oil-prices
"We will first discuss with menteris besar and all party leaders. On the proposal for a special parliamentary sitting, we need a clear plan to address the issue before bringing it to Parliament," he said.
On a related matter, Anwar reiterated Malaysia's support for peace efforts involving Iran following the conflict in the Middle East, stressing the need for a just resolution for all parties.
He said extensive discussions had been held with the Iranian president, who expressed commitment towards pursuing peace, while Malaysia maintains its position that the root causes of the conflict must be acknowledged, including initial attacks involving Israel and the United States.
He said the conflict has not only affected the region but has also had spillover effects on other countries, including Malaysia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: In London's pubs, love gets a PowerPoint makeover
Article:
LONDON: Fed up of swiping left or right on dating apps, young London singles are returning to a classic British way of meeting people – in the pub – with a modern twist: a PowerPoint presentation by a close friend pitching them as dating material.
Putting together a slide deck is second nature for many younger millennials and older Gen Zers. Now, they're using that skill to enhance their mates' love lives, just as dating apps are losing their appeal.
"I hate the swiping," said Annie, 27, adding she was keen to see if her friend's two-minute pitch at a 'Date my Mate' event could land her a boyfriend–or at least a first date.
Organisers said all 150 tickets for the event, held at a north London pub, were sold in less than five minutes.
And they're planning to roll out more, aiming for one a week across the country.
Dating apps lose shine
Dating apps are fading in popularity in the UK, with usage across the 10 biggest platforms falling 16% between 2023 and 2024, according to Global Dating Insights.
Polling shows nearly half of users have had negative experiences on apps like Tinder and Hinge.
At the event, friends crowded around tables with glasses of wine, wearing "Date" or "Mate" stickers, as presenters clicked through slides of flattering holiday snaps and fun facts.
"A bit more about Lauren. She's 30. She's a structural engineer, so even though she will stop walls from falling down, she'll always be there to support you," said one friend.
"Interesting fact, she's just about to submit her sexy fantasy romance book to publishers."
Having seen similar events in Australia and the US on social media, Emily Churchill, head of marketing at wine company Nice who helped arrange the event, said she knew she had to bring the concept to London.
"It's just so much fun," she said, adding it gave coupled-up friends a way to help their single mates find love and escape "horrible dating stories."
'Vintage' way of meeting people
Those hunting for a match said it was easier for a friend to sell their best qualities–and poke fun at their flaws–with humour and honesty.
"It's quite retro. It's a vintage way of meeting people," said Jonny, 28, a journalist, joking about how he tried to steer his own presentation.
His friend Patrick said the preparation involved "hundreds of photos over WhatsApp, hundreds, and him being incredibly specific about the ones that make him look good."
Laughing about whether the friendship would survive the pitch, Patrick said he was confident the slides were good enough to lead to a wedding some day. — AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RON95 ban on foreign vehicles starts April 1: What you need to know
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Starting April 1, enforcement of the ban on the purchase of subsidised RON95 petrol by foreign-registered vehicles will take effect.
Here's what you need to know:
What is the ban about?
The ban prohibits all foreign-registered vehicles from purchasing RON95 petrol, which is subsidised by the government for Malaysians.
What has changed?
Previously, enforcement action was largely taken against petrol station operators found selling RON95 to foreign-registered vehicles.
Under the expanded rules, drivers and owners of foreign-registered vehicles can now also face action if they are caught purchasing the subsidised fuel.
Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the move follows a policy
decision
presented in Parliament on Jan 29.
It is part of Op Tiris 4.0 (Integrated), a nationwide operation to curb the misuse and smuggling of subsidised fuel, particularly in border areas.
Who will be affected?
*Drivers and owners of foreign-registered vehicles
*Petrol station operators who fail to comply with regulations.
How can the public report violations?
The public can channel complaints or provide information to the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry via:
WhatsApp
: 019-848 8000
e-Aduan
portal: https://eaduan.kpdn.gov.my
Call centre
: 1-800-886-800
Ez ADU KPDN mobile application
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Maduro makes second appearance at New York courthouse
Article:
NEW YORK: Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro made his second appearance in a New York court on Thursday morning following his forcible seizure by United States (US) troops during a military raid in Caracas in early January, reported Xinhua.
During his first arraignment on Jan 5, Maduro pleaded not guilty to all US charges against him, including drug trafficking, and told the court that he was "kidnapped" from his Caracas residence and that he remains president of Venezuela.
On Thursday, Maduro's lawyer continued pushing the judge to throw out the charges in court and argued that the US is violating Maduro's constitutional rights by blocking Venezuelan government funds from paying for his legal fees.
Although the judge questioned Washington's rationale for barring Maduro from using Venezuelan funds to pay for his legal defence, he denied the call for the case to be dismissed.
On Jan 3, US military forces launched a large-scale strike against Venezuela and forcibly seized Maduro and his wife, Cilia Flores, before transporting them to New York.
The US attacks have shocked the international community, triggering a stream of condemnation and serious concerns worldwide.
The couple has been jailed at the Metropolitan Detention Centre in Brooklyn, New York, since their seizure in Caracas.
Protesters gathered outside the courthouse, which was heavily guarded by law enforcement officers, chanting slogans and demanding that Maduro be freed immediately.
"We're here today standing with Nicholas Maduro and Cilia Flores," Jacoe Muldoon, a young protester, told Xinhua.
"We think charges should be dismissed and that they should be freed."
Another protester said that the US had no right to kidnap an elected leader of another country, adding that the Venezuelan people are united to face the challenges.
In Muldoon's eyes, the situation in Venezuela and that in Iran are definitely connected as the US launched aggression overseas to seize resources globally. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: School closure allowed if temperatures exceed 37 °C for 3 days
Article:
IPOH: School administrators may temporarily close schools if temperatures exceed 37 degrees Celsius for three consecutive days.
Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said the measure is part of guidelines set by the Education Ministry since 2023 and can be implemented if hot and dry conditions worsen.
"State Education Departments and schools have sufficient experience in managing the current situation.
"We recognise this remains a challenge for the ministry in ensuring the comfort and health of students," he said after a working visit to SJKT Gopeng here today.
Also present were Perak Human Resources, Health, Indian Community Affairs and National Integration Committee chairman A. Sivanesan and State Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Sandrea Ng Shy Ching.
"Outdoor school activities must also be suspended if temperatures range between 35 and 37 degrees Celsius for three consecutive days, including cross-country runs, camping, drills and other outdoor activities," he said.
The ministry has instructed all administrators, including principals, headmasters and District Education Offices, to monitor daily temperature levels at schools and within their respective districts.
In another development, Wong said SJKT Gopeng will undergo redevelopment under a RM14.5 million project to replace dilapidated buildings.
He said the project is expected to begin at the end of this year and will take 22 months to complete.
It involves demolishing the existing structures and constructing a new three-storey block comprising six classrooms, administrative offices, a staff room and a sports store.
"The school is 80 years old and its main classroom and administrative blocks have been affected by termite infestation.
"If all goes as planned, the project is expected to be completed by the end of 2028, allowing students to move into the new building," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Govt to explore all options to keep RON95 subsidy beyond May
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The government will explore all available avenues to sustain the Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) petroleum subsidy scheme beyond May, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
He said Putrajaya would leverage all available resources, including Petroliam Nasional Bhd's (Petronas) global investments, to secure alternative petroleum sources from countries such as Brazil, Suriname and Canada.
Anwar reassured that the government would maintain the subsidy until May.
"For now, we can sustain it until May, and we will continue to explore (alternative fuel sources).
"The speciality of Petronas lies in its diversified investments, not only in the Gulf region, but also in Brazil, Suriname and Canada.
"We must find ways to ensure returns and help meet domestic needs," he told reporters after Friday prayers at Masjid Tengku Ampuan Jemaah here today.
His remarks follow the government's decision to temporarily reduce the Budi95
monthly purchase limit
from RM300 to RM200, effective April 1, in light of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.
Anwar said Malaysia remains in a more stable position compared with some neighbouring countries.
"We are fortunate that our situation is not as severe as that faced by others. There is no rationing and no sharp increase in fuel prices — RON95 remains unchanged, and supply is assured until May. We are monitoring the situation daily.
"We must not become complacent or be overly distracted by politics. Instead, we must remain focused on safeguarding the welfare of the people and the country," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: National Integrated Immigration System to roll out in phases from March 31
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The National Integrated Immigration System (NIISe) will be rolled out in phases across all air, land, and sea checkpoints starting March 31.
The Home Ministry said the implementation of the system is a strategic move to strengthen border security through a digitalised immigration framework that is more efficient, faster, and user-friendly.
The rollout will begin with a pilot phase at selected locations, starting with Senai International Airport in Johor (air mode) on March 31, followed by the Bukit Bunga ICQS Complex (land mode) on May 31, and the Puteri Harbour International Ferry Terminal (sea mode) on June 30, the ministry said in a statement today.
The phased implementation follows a successful trial period conducted from September 2025 to February, which confirmed the system's readiness for nationwide expansion.
During the trial phase, several high-impact initiatives were launched, including the MyNIISe mobile application and the installation of eGates at major international airports.
Other developments included the implementation of QR code systems at the Sultan Iskandar Building (BSI) and Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB), the activation of the Advance Passenger Screening System, and the establishment of National Immigration Command Centres in Putrajaya, Kuching, and Kota Kinabalu.
The ministry noted that as of March 17, the MyNIISe app had recorded 1,780,425 downloads with 897,673 registered users, including 616,730 Malaysians and 246,946 Singaporeans.
Furthermore, the QR code system at BSI and KSAB has recorded millions of transactions, proving the system's capability to manage traveller flow rapidly and efficiently, it said.
The ministry added that NIISe is part of the Madani government's commitment to strengthening national security and enhancing immigration service delivery through comprehensive digitalisation.
The public is encouraged to download the MyNIISe application early to ensure smooth immigration clearance as the full implementation progresses.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AMS Advanced Material eyes RM32.77mil from IPO ahead of ACE Market listing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: AMS Advanced Material Bhd aims to raise RM32.77 million from its initial public offering (IPO) ahead of its listing on the ACE Market on April 23.
The aluminium specialist company's IPO entails a public issue of 113 million new ordinary shares at 29 sen per share, representing 18.46 per cent of its enlarged issued share capital of 612.00 million shares.
The IPO also comprises an offer for sale of 47 million existing ordinary shares through private placement to selected investors.
Of the total proceeds raised from the exercise, 24.73 per cent will be allocated for the setting up of a new licensed manufacturing warehouse plant in Penang to support its export-orientated semiconductor and engineering customers.
In addition, 21.05 per cent will be utilised to expand into the manufacturing of aluminium architectural products to broaden its product offerings.
Another 7.63 per cent will be allocated for the establishment of a new distribution point in Kuantan, Pahang, to enhance delivery efficiency and market reach across the East Coast region.
A further 24.92 per cent will be earmarked for the repayment of borrowings, and 8.15 per cent will be set aside for working capital to support the day-to-day operations.
The remaining proceeds will be used to cover IPO and listing-related expenses.
AMS managing director Keh Teng Yang said the company has steadily built a solid foundation, allowing it to transform beyond a pure trading business and expand into higher value-added activities.
"This progression has enabled us to move up the value chain and better serve customers. Today, AMS has grown into an established supplier of semi-finished aluminium and copper products in Malaysia, offering a wide range of products across various dimensions and grades.
"From a modest trading operation, we have evolved into a trusted partner supporting industrial players across multiple sectors in the region," he added.
The public application for the IPO shares opens today and closes at 5pm on April 10.
M & A Securities Sdn Bhd is the principal adviser, sponsor, underwriter and placement agent for the IPO.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit retreats to 4.00 versus US dollar amid Middle East ceasefire uncertainties
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit retreated to the 4.00 level against the US dollar at noon, as risk-off mode dominated market sentiment due to growing scepticism over a ceasefire in Iran after United States (US) President Donald Trump indicated that he would not commit to a deal with Tehran.
"Following the announcement, the US Dollar Index (DXY) was seen climbing 0.28 per cent to 99.873 points, holding steady as safe-haven demand, while US benchmark equities indices fell more than one per cent," said Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd's chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid.
The local unit had opened at  3.9855 versus the greenback.
At 12.30pm,  the ringgit stood at 4.0075/0105 versus the US dollar, marking its lowest position in two months, when it stood at 4.0045 on Jan 23.
Xinhua also reported that financial markets continued to be heavily influenced by the hostilities involving the US, Israel, and Iran.
Both Tehran and Tel Aviv launched military strikes on Thursday amid growing international warnings about the conflict's severe economic costs.
However, equities managed to briefly pare some of their steep morning losses following reports that Iran had formally responded to a US truce proposal, the report said.
The ringgit also traded softer against a basket of other major currencies.
It slid versus the Japanese yen to 2.5114/5135 from 2.4951/5077 at Friday's opening, fell against the British pound to 5.3460/3500 from 5.3143/3403 earlier and dropped vis-à-vis the euro to 4.6243/6277from 4.5969/6194 previously.
Against Asean currencies, it eased versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1177/1203 from 3.0994/1148 at the opening, slipped versus the Thai baht to 12.1942/2085 from 12.0868/1566 this morning, edged down vis-a-vis the Indonesian rupiah to 236.5/236.7 from 235.7/237.0 ealier, and inched down against the Philippine peso to 6.64/6.65 from 6.61/6.65 previously.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Father longs for daughter jailed in China, especially during Raya
Article:
PASIR PUTEH: For almost two decades, former soldier Mohamad Lazim Jusoh has celebrated Hari Raya Aidilfitri without his eldest daughter, Umi Azlim, 43, who has been imprisoned in China.
Hari Raya has never felt the same since May 2007, when Umi Azlim, then 24, was arrested in China, Lazim said.
"Every Raya, she would be the busiest in the kitchen with her mother, preparing all our favourite dishes," he recalled, his voice tinged with longing.
Among the meals the family misses most are Umi Azlim's lontong, nasi himpit and mee soto — dishes that once brought everyone together in their home.
"This year marks the 19th time we are celebrating without her. We truly miss her, especially her cooking and her presence during Raya," Lazim said.
Umi Azlim studied biology at Universiti Malaysia Sabah.
Umi Azlim, the eldest of six siblings, was initially sentenced to death in China for attempting to smuggle heroin.
Her family insists she was an unwitting mule, allegedly deceived by a drug syndicate while working for a Kuala Lumpur-based company marketing foot massage devices there.
After a review by the Supreme People's Court, her sentence was commuted to life imprisonment.
She is now serving her term at Guangzhou Prison.
Life imprisonment in China comes with the possibility of eventual release.
Umi Azlim was arrested in Shantou, a coastal city in Guangdong province in May, 2007.
Lazim said her absence is deeply felt across the family, especially by her younger sister, Umi Zulaikha, 34, who remains particularly close to her.
"Despite the years apart, we still hold onto hope," he said.
"We are counting the days until she is released. There are four more years to go. By then, she will be 47," Lazim said.
"Time has passed, but our love and longing have never faded. Each Raya serves as a poignant reminder of the daughter we are still waiting to welcome home," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Budi95 quota adjustment won't affect 90 per cent of Malaysians, says Saifuddin
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Nine out of 10 Malaysians will not be affected by the upcoming Budi95 quota adjustment, as the majority of consumers use only about 100 litres of petrol monthly.
Pakatan Harapan (PH) secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said daily life for 90 per cent of the population would continue as normal without additional costs.
Saifuddin, who is also the home minister, said the temporary adjustment of the Budi95 eligibility limit from 300 litres to 200 litres a month, effective April 1, would remain in place until global conditions stabilised.
He assured e-hailing drivers that their quota remained at 800 litres a month to protect their livelihoods.
Saifuddin said the national fuel subsidy bill had surged sixfold from RM700 million to RM4 billion a month due to the conflict in the Middle East, forcing the government to balance the figures through the temporary quota adjustment.
"The Madani government maintains a clear stance that the people must be protected by continuing the Budi95 subsidy despite increasingly challenging global pressures," he said in a statement today.
He added that while other nations had passed the burden to their citizens, the Malaysian government had chosen to absorb the price hikes to prevent a drastic impact on the cost of living.
"Today's world is facing increasingly challenging global pressures. The surge in crude oil prices past US$100 per barrel has had a major impact on many countries.
"In Hong Kong, petrol prices have reached around RM14 per litre. In Singapore, it is about RM10 per litre. This is the reality the world is facing today. However, in Malaysia, the Madani government under the leadership of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has taken a clear stand to protect the people," he said.
Saifuddin said the country must make wise choices as a "big family" and not allow RM4 billion of the people's money to 'burn' on the roads every month.
He said the savings from the subsidy rationalisation were needed to build more schools, upgrade hospitals, and provide direct cash assistance to those in need.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar arrives in Indonesia for talks with Prabowo on Middle East crisis and bilateral ties
Article:
JAKARTA, March 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim arrived in Jakarta on Friday afternoon for a special visit to discuss escalating geopolitical developments in West Asia and strengthen Malaysia-Indonesia bilateral cooperation.
The aircraft carrying Anwar landed at Halim Perdanakusuma Airport in East Jakarta at 3.45pm local time.
The Prime Minister was received by Indonesian Foreign Minister Sugiono, Indonesia’s Ambassador to Malaysia Raden Datuk Mohammad Iman Hascarya and Malaysia’s Ambassador to Indonesia Datuk Syed Mohamad Hasrin Tengku Hussin.
Anwar was also greeted by a guard of honour mounted by the Presidential Security Forces at the airport, before being escorted to the Istana Merdeka in Central Jakarta, where he is scheduled to meet Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto.
Discussions between the two leaders later are expected to focus on geopolitical developments in West Asia, including the situations in Palestine and Iran, as well as their implications for regional and global stability.
This is Anwar’s first trip to Jakarta this year at the invitation of Prabowo, following a phone conversation between the two leaders on March 23.
Both leaders are also expected to streamline bilateral cooperation ahead of Malaysia hosting the 14th Malaysia-Indonesia Annual Consultation later this year.
Meanwhile, Malaysia’s Foreign Ministry said the meeting will also underscore the importance of strengthening Asean’s role as a united and principled bloc, as well as its ability to act collectively in safeguarding regional peace and stability and ensuring economic resilience amid global uncertainty. — Bernama

Title: Karambunai state polls result stands as Election Court throws out Warisan challenge
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, March 27 — The Election Court on Friday struck out a Warisan candidate’s election petition seeking to nullify the Karambunai constituency election result in the 17th Sabah state election.
High Court judge Wong Siong Tung, via Zoom, allowed all preliminary objections raised by the respondents to strike out the election petition filed against them.
The petitioner, Ahmad Jais Otong, named the returning officer (RO) for the Karambunai state constituency election, the Election Commission (EC) of Malaysia and Perikatan Nasional’s PAS candidate, Datuk Aliakbar Gulasan, as the first, second and third respondents, respectively.
The petitioner was ordered to pay costs of RM15,000 to the first and second respondents jointly, and a further RM15,000 to the third respondent.
Aliakbar won the 11-cornered contest in Karambunai with 7,054 votes and a majority of 365 votes, while Ahmad received 6,689 votes. — Daily Express

Title: English text of Federal Constitution prevails until Agong says otherwise, Putrajaya tells courts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — The English version of the Federal Constitution is the nation’s authoritative legal document until the Yang di-Pertuan Agong declares otherwise, Putrajaya has asserted in its official defence against a lawsuit seeking to elevate the Bahasa Melayu version.
The government stated that the Agong has not yet prescribed the Bahasa Melayu text as supreme under Article 160B of the constitution, which allows for such a declaration to be made,
Free Malaysia Today
reported.
It cited two landmark Federal Court rulings in the cases of Indira Gandhi and Loh Siew Hong as binding precedents that have already settled the matter.
“The English version of the constitution is the one that prevails,” the government said in its statement of defence.
The lawsuit was filed last year by lawyer Haniff Khatri Abdulla, who is seeking a court declaration that the Bahasa Melayu version of the constitution should take precedence.
Haniff argued that although a translated Malay version was launched in 2003, the government has since failed in its constitutional duty to dignify the national language by not taking the necessary steps to make it the authoritative text.
In its defence, the government noted that Haniff had represented the Perlis Islamic religious and Malay customs council (MAIPs) in the Loh Siew Hong case.
It suggested the present lawsuit was brought due to his dissatisfaction with the Federal Court’s ruling in that case, which struck down the unilateral conversion of Loh’s three children based on the English text’s interpretation of the word “parent.”
In both the Indira Gandhi and Loh Siew Hong cases, the apex court held that the word “parent” in the English version required the consent of both parents for a minor’s conversion to Islam, overriding interpretations of the Malay text.
The case is scheduled for a four-day hearing at the High Court here, beginning on May 24, 2027.

Title: Driver dies after Isuzu D-Max plunges into drain in Sandakan crash
Article:
SANDAKAN, March 27 — A man died after the four-wheel-drive vehicle he was driving skidded and plunged into a drain in a road accident in front of the Federal Building Office at Batu 7 here on Friday afternoon.
The victim, identified as Chock Vui Heong, 50, was pronounced dead at the scene by medical personnel.
Sandakan Fire and Rescue Station chief Jimmy Lagung said the department received an emergency call at 12.56pm before a team was dispatched to the location about seven kilometres away.
He said firefighters arrived at 1.05pm and found an Isuzu D-Max believed to have skidded on its own before crashing into the drain, with the victim trapped inside.
He said the victim was extricated using special equipment, including hydraulic rescue tools, but was confirmed dead at the scene before the body was sent to hospital, while operations ended at 1.58pm after ensuring the area was safe. — Daily Express

Title: KK court fixes May 25 mention in Shafie Apdal contempt proceedings linked to Zara Qairina inquest
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, March 27 — The Sessions Court has fixed May 25 for a mention in the committal proceedings involving former chief minister Datuk Seri Mohd Shafie Apdal to receive an update on a judicial review application seeking to quash the inquest into the death of Zara Qairina Mahathir.
Judge Amir Shah Amir Hassan set the date during a Zoom mention of the case on Friday.
Shafie is alleged to have made remarks concerning the inquest that may amount to contempt of court, while the court had previously granted a stay of the committal proceedings pending updates on the judicial review.
Deputy public prosecutor Christopher Enteri Mawan said the court was informed that the judicial review in the High Court remains ongoing and parties were directed to update its status at the next mention, including discussions on constitutional issues related to an ordinance and its impact on federal and state law.
The court had earlier granted the Attorney General leave to commence contempt proceedings against Shafie and activist Che Muna Din, while a judicial review application filed by Pertubuhan Kebajikan Ekonomi Semangat Bersatu Malaysia challenging the inquest proceedings is opposed by the Attorney General’s Chambers, with respective counsel representing the parties. — Daily Express

Title: MetMalaysia issues storm alert for parts of Sabah and Sarawak until 6pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in Sabah and Sarawak until 6pm today.
MetMalaysia said in a statement that Kapit (Belaga) and Limbang in Sarawak are affected.
Meanwhile, the Interior (Sipitang and Beaufort) and West Coast (Papar and Penampang) in Sabah are also affected. — Bernama

Title: Sibu man jailed four months for slashing girlfriend’s ex-husband with knife
Article:
SIBU, March 27 — The Magistrates’ Court here has sentenced a 23-year-old man to four months in prison for injuring his girlfriend’s ex-husband with a knife.
Magistrate Romario Jonoi meted out the sentence after Maxwell Milak pleaded guilty to a charge under Section 324 of the Penal Code, which provides for imprisonment of up to 10 years, or a fine, or caning, or any combination thereof, upon conviction.
Romario also ordered for the sentence to run from the date of Maxwell’s conviction.
Maxwell was charged with intentionally causing injury to his girlfriend’s 32-year-old ex-husband using a knife on March 21, 2026 at 6.48pm at the compound of a house on Jalan Chong Sang.
Based on the facts of the case, on the day of the incident, the complainant had gone to the house to meet his children.
However, upon arrival, the complainant got into an argument with Maxwell, who is said to be the boyfriend of the complainant’s ex-wife.
During the altercation, Maxwell injured the complainant on the cheek using a sharp weapon.
On March 22, at around 1am, police arrested Maxwell at the same house to assist with investigations and seized a knife measuring 19cm.
Investigations revealed that the complainant had first punched Maxwell in the face, which then caused him to retaliate by swinging the knife towards the complainant’s right cheek and injuring him.
ASP Sokolinkam Changgan handled the prosecution, while Maxwell did not have legal representation. — The Borneo Post

Title: Fire rips through 11 homes in Batu Ferringhi, residents escape unhurt
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, March 27 — Residents of 11 houses in Kampung Din Manbo, Batu Ferringhi, here, went through anxious moments when their homes were destroyed in a fire this morning.
Penang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant director (Operations) John Sagun Francis said 23 personnel with seven fire engines from the Teluk Bahang, Jalan Perak and Bayan Baru fire stations were dispatched to the scene after receiving an emergency call at 11.29am.
He said no casualties were reported in the incident.
“The fire involved 11 double-storey semi-permanent houses (Class B), with structural damage estimated at about 80 per cent.
“Firefighting operations were challenging due to narrow access routes and low water pressure at the location,” he said in a statement today.
John said the fire was brought under control at 12.21pm, adding that investigations are ongoing to determine the cause and total losses. — Bernama

Title: Education Ministry reinforces safety measures in schools after King’s heat warning
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, March 27 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) upholds the decree of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, to safeguard the safety and well-being of students, teachers and the school community, amid the current hot weather.
In a statement today, the MOE said that the King’s decree serves as a reminder to all educational institutions under the ministry to take precautionary measures and to always adhere to guidelines in dealing with the ongoing hot and dry weather conditions.
“The MOE has issued relevant instructions to all institutions under its administration.
“This includes the need to monitor the implementation of outdoor activities, as well as granting special permission to students, teachers and school support staff to wear appropriate sports attire during hot and dry weather,” it said.
Yesterday, in a post on the Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar, His Majesty emphasised the importance of safeguarding health by reducing outdoor activities exposed to hot and dry weather, and ensuring adequate water intake throughout the period.
Sultan Ibrahim also said that the MOE should give serious attention to the matter, to ensure that the health of students is not adversely affected by the hot weather phenomenon. — Bernama

Title: Over 300kg of subsidised cooking oil seized in raid on Pasir Mas syndicate, says Kelantan Domestic Trade Ministry
Article:
KOTA BHARU, March 27 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) busted a syndicate for misappropriating over 300kg of subsidised cooking oil in Kampung Lubuk Tapah, Pasir Mas, yesterday.
Kelantan KPDN deputy director Aswadi Jaafar said a 3.45pm raid on a house believed to be used as a transit point before smuggling led to the arrest of three local men aged 27, 28 and 44.
“Initial intelligence spotted a white Toyota Hiace van with a local registration, believed to be used by the syndicate to deliver supplies.
“…the suspects were caught moving boxes of subsidised cooking oil outside a house thought to be a transit point before the supplies were sold on the black market,” he said in a statement today.
He said 12 boxes of subsidised cooking oil (204kg) were found outside the house, with another 119kg inside, totalling 323kg seized.
“…the case is being investigated under the Control of Supplies Act 1961,” he said, adding that all the suspects had been taken to the KPDN office for further action. — Bernama

Title: Election Court strikes out Warisan petition over Pitas state seat, orders RM30,000 in costs
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, March 27 — The Election Court has struck out a petition filed by Warisan candidate Datuk Harun Ismail challenging the outcome of the Pitas state seat, which was won by Gabungan Rakyat Sabah’s Datuk Seri Ruddy Awah.
Kuching-based High Court judge Wong Siong Tung, who sat as the election court judge via a Zoom proceeding, delivered the decision after allowing preliminary objections raised by the respondents.
When contacted, Ruddy’s counsel Shahlan Jufri said the court also ordered the petitioner to pay costs of RM15,000 to his client and a further RM15,000 jointly to the second and third respondents.
Shahlan appeared together with Rizwandean Bukhary M Burhan, Rupilin Aliq, and Mohd Luqman Syazwan Zabidi Md Zain.
Ruddy is currently Assistant Minister of Works and Utilities.
Harun, 66, filed the election petition on December 23 last year, naming Ruddy, 57, as the first respondent, Pitas returning officer as the second respondent, and the Election Commission as the third respondent.
In the petition, Harun sought a declaration that Ruddy was not duly elected as Pitas assemblyman and that the state election held on November 29, 2025 be declared null and void, along with costs and other relief deemed fit by the court.
Earlier this year, the respondents filed preliminary objections to strike out the petition, citing, among others, insufficient particulars in the allegations of bribery and a lack of concise material facts to support claims of electoral misconduct.
The second and third respondents were represented by senior federal counsel Shamsul Bolhassan, alongside federal counsels Safiyyah Omar, Nur Akmal Mohamad Azri, and Nurul Muhaimin Mohd Azman.
Harun was represented by lawyer Hairul Vaiyron Othman.
In the Sabah state election, Ruddy won the Pitas seat in a six-cornered contest, securing 5,754 votes and a majority of 1,565, while Harun garnered 4,189 votes. — The Borneo Post

Title: Johor Bomba turns to aerial firefighting as winds reignite sections of 150-hectare fire
Article:
KOTA TINGGI, March 27 — An MI-17 helicopter of the Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Air Unit will begin water bombing operations to extinguish the fire on Jalan Bypass Punggai-Sebana Cove, here.
According to a Johor JBPM statement, the initiative will be implemented after the same method has been completed in extinguishing a fire near the Pengerang People’s Housing Project (PPR).
JBPM said the extinguishing operation is still continuing in section G by ‘direct attack’ and ‘total flooding’ using ‘water tankers’ to control the spread of the fire.
The agency informed that excavators would continue to carry out ‘fire breaks’ in the section concerned with the planned extinguishing efforts also covering water sources from a Yayasan Perladangan Johor pond to boost fire control.
“90 per cent or 135 hectares of the 150 hectares of area involved in the fire on Jalan Bypass Punggai-Sebana Cove, here, have been successfully extinguished as of 9 am,” he said today.
For the fire near PPR Pengerang, 55 per cent or 5.5 hectares of the 10 hectares affected area were extinguished using the ‘direct attack’ and ‘total flooding’ methods in sections E, F and C, with water from nearby fire hydrants.
However, the fire has spread back to section C due to strong winds, hence, the same helicopter will carry out water bombing operations in ‘sections E and G’.
“Meanwhile, excavators will continue to carry out ‘fire breaks’ in sections F and G to extinguish the remaining 4.5 hectares,” he said.
